name: Fat-specific insulin receptor knockout extended mouse lifespan
summary: FIRKO mice provide animal lifespan support for tissue-specific insulin signaling attenuation.
statement: Mice lacking insulin receptor specifically in adipose tissue had reduced fat mass, protection from age-related obesity and metabolic abnormalities, and increased mean lifespan.
quantitative_note: The source reports approximately 18% mean lifespan extension.
caveats: This is germline tissue-specific mouse genetics, not a practical human intervention.
Adipose insulin receptor deletion may not map cleanly to systemic insulin lowering or glucose-spike control.
Mouse lifespan evidence remains indirect for human aging.
